meta.title: Track record — public, forever | BetsPlug
meta.description: Every BetsPlug prediction is locked before kickoff and stays in our public record forever — wins and losses both. See how the lock mechanism works.

hero.subheadline: Most prediction sites hide their losses. We don't. Every BetsPlug prediction is locked before kickoff and stays in our public record forever — wins and losses both.

whyTransparency.h2: The prediction industry has a {{accent}}credibility problem{{/accent}}
paragraphs: Most football prediction sites do something simple to look reliable: they quietly remove their losing predictions. After a few weeks of selective publishing, their 'track record' shows mostly wins. The result: bettors who follow these sites lose money based on misleading information.

Some sites go further. They edit predictions after kickoff to match the actual result. They claim '85% accuracy' without any way for users to verify. They use testimonials from accounts that don't exist. The entire industry has a trust problem because there's no mechanism to prove or disprove these claims.

We rejected this approach from day one. Not because we're more honest than competitors — but because we built our system to make manipulation impossible. The transparency isn't a marketing claim. It's how the platform works.

pillars.subH2: Three mechanisms make our track record verifiable, not just claimed.

items.title: Locked Timestamps
items.body: Every prediction is committed to our database with a public timestamp before kickoff. After this moment, the prediction cannot be edited, deleted, or replaced — not by us, not by anyone. This isn't policy. It's architectural. The system doesn't have an 'edit' function for locked predictions.

items.title: Wins and Losses Both
items.body: We publish every prediction we generate. Wins, losses, and pushes all go into the same public record. There's no curation, no selective showing. If our model is wrong about a match, that loss is permanently visible alongside our wins. Cherry-picking is impossible because nothing is hidden.

items.title: Methodology Public
items.body: Our prediction methodology is fully documented: which statistical models we use (Elo + Poisson), which machine learning approach applies, which 40+ variables go into each prediction. No 'secret algorithm' claims. You can read the entire methodology and verify that the same logic applied to every match in our track record.

whatYouCanVerify.subH2: Once a prediction is generated, the system records it with a public timestamp. After this moment, no one — not even us — can modify it.

features.text: Prediction generated and committed to database
Public timestamp records the exact moment
Database has no edit function for locked records
Original prediction stays visible forever — win or lose
Aggregate patterns become visible in our methodology data

whatYouCanVerify.noteUnderCta: Our methodology page explains the technical guarantees in detail.

paragraphs: Our track record is the proof behind our methodology. If you don't know how the predictions are generated, you can't evaluate whether the track record means anything. That's why we publish both: the methodology that creates predictions, and the historical record of how those predictions performed.

Read the full methodology to understand what variables we process, which models we combine, and why we lock predictions before kickoff. Then see today's predictions to watch the same approach applied in real time.

questions.q: Where can I see today's predictions?
questions.a: Today's predictions live on our /predictions page — free preview every day, with full daily picks behind the subscription. The lock mechanism is the same: every prediction is committed with a public timestamp before kickoff.

questions.q: Can predictions really not be edited after publication?
questions.a: Correct. Once a prediction is locked with its kickoff timestamp, our system has no mechanism to modify it. This is built into the architecture, not a policy we promise to follow. Even we cannot change locked predictions.

questions.q: How do I verify a specific prediction was locked before kickoff?
questions.a: Each published prediction carries a lock timestamp — the exact moment it was committed. Compare this with the actual match kickoff time. The lock timestamp is always before kickoff.

questions.q: Why don't you show specific accuracy percentages on this page?
questions.a: Accuracy varies dramatically by league, market type, and time period. A single headline percentage hides this variance. Our methodology page shows performance in context — which competitions, which markets, which periods we've performed best and worst.

questions.q: Do I need to subscribe to see how the system works?
questions.a: No. The full methodology is public — read /methodology to understand exactly how predictions are generated and locked. A subscription unlocks daily predictions; understanding the process is free.

questions.q: What stops you from removing losing predictions?
questions.a: Architecture. Our database doesn't have a 'delete locked prediction' function. We literally cannot remove predictions once they're locked. This was an intentional design choice when we built the platform — to make the track record provably immutable, not just policy-protected.

finalCta.body: You've seen how we guarantee transparency. The next step is to see the predictions our methodology produces — and decide whether they're worth your subscription.
